Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, including serving as **post-sale technical lead** for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract, managing stakeholder escalations to senior executives.

Built an internal AI agent and restructured planning workflows to reduce **planning cycle time by 39%** and increase drill capacity by 21%, applying automation to accelerate production readiness across the largest and most complex customer accounts.

Orchestrated cross-functional delivery across engineering, support, and partner teams — creating use-case execution plans, tracking **KPI health metrics**, and ensuring platform adoption milestones were met on schedule for strategic accounts.

Defined and drove technical account strategy for high-priority Databricks and SAP partner integrations, translating ambiguous requirements into structured delivery roadmaps that **accelerated workload onboarding** and maximized return on platform investment.

Drove a discovery-led product strategy to eliminate manual toil during region launches — conducting structured interviews with 20+ service teams to identify **81 hours of manual effort per region** and building a roadmap to automate critical execution paths.

Enabled leadership to identify automation gaps and **prioritize high-impact workloads** by engineering a Power BI dashboard that tracked operational toil across 140+ teams, providing the KPI visibility needed for data-driven investment decisions.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets**, stabilize the developer intake process, and maintain delivery momentum across a complex, multi-team program.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and boosted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy — optimizing the website and Google profile to improve lead quality and lower cost per acquired account.

tailoring_notes: 'Post-sale technical lead' (JD term) woven into microsoft_ft bullet#4 and microsoft_ft bullet#7 to mirror the DSA post-win accountability framing.
'Use-case execution plan' and 'go-live' (JD terms) embedded in microsoft_ft bullets #2 and #6 to reflect the DSA's core motion of moving workloads from win to production.
'KPI health metrics' and 'platform adoption' (JD terms) surfaced in microsoft_ft bullet#6 to match the JD's explicit requirement for internal/external KPI reporting on usage and customer health.
'Discovery workshops' (JD term) used in microsoft_2023 bullet#2 to align with the JD's call for open-ended discovery and business analysis skills.
'Accelerated workload onboarding' and 'return on platform investment' (JD language) placed in microsoft_ft bullet#7 to echo the DSA mission of maximizing customer ROI on Databricks consumption.
